messages.content:
I want to create an email, the email is about stating that some of my requirements are still currently unavailable. The birth certificate that I currently have is also only a photocopied version as I lost my previous original copy of the said document. As for SSS document, I already have one but there is a correction that I still need to do in the office of SSS. Pagibig document is being processed I only still have the Registration tracking number and I still dont have the full member form/MDF. As for PhilHealth and NBI documents, I will try to get it on Monday as offices are not available on weekends. The only Available document I have currently is the TIN document. 
Make this email as respectful as possible as I am talking to the HR of my newly hired company.

Subject: Update on Required Documents Submission

Dear [HR Manager's Name],

I hope this message finds you well. I am writing to provide an update regarding the status of the required documents as part of my employment onboarding process. I am committed to ensuring that all necessary paperwork is completed and submitted promptly. However, I wish to inform you that there are currently some items that are unavailable or in the process of being obtained or corrected.

Firstly, I would like to address the issue regarding my birth certificate. Unfortunately, I lost my original copy and currently only possess a photocopy of the document. I understand the importance of submitting the original document and am exploring the necessary steps to obtain a new certified copy at the earliest.

Regarding the SSS document, I have already secured one. However, there is a discrepancy that needs correction. I plan to visit the SSS office to rectify this matter and ensure the document accurately reflects my personal details.

As for the Pag-IBIG document, it is currently being processed. At this stage, I only have the Registration Tracking Number, and I am yet to receive the full Member's Data Form (MDF). I am closely following up on this process to acquire the complete document as soon as possible.

I am also aware of the requirement for the PhilHealth and NBI documents. Given that the offices are not available over the weekend, I intend to process these documents first thing on Monday.

The only document I currently have readily available is the TIN document, which I am prepared to submit at your earliest convenience.

I understand the importance of these documents in finalizing my employment details and assure you of my best efforts to complete and submit the remaining documents at the soonest possible time. I appreciate your understanding and patience as I work through these matters.

Please let me know if there are any alternative arrangements that can be considered in the meantime, or if you require any further information from my side.

Thank you for your attention to this matter and for your ongoing support as I make these arrangements. I am looking forward to starting my journey with [Company Name] and am eager to contribute to our mutual success.

Warm regards,

[Your Name]
[Your Contact Information]
If I don't know the name should I just greet?

Absolutely, if you're not aware of the HR manager's name or the specific person you're addressing, it's acceptable to use a general greeting. In such cases, you can use a polite and professional salutation that doesn't require a specific name. Here's how you might start your email under those circumstances:

Subject: Update on Required Documents Submission

Dear HR Team,

I hope this message finds you well. I am writing to provide an update regarding the status of the required documents as part of my employment onboarding process. I am fully committed to ensuring that all necessary paperwork is completed and submitted promptly. However, I wish to inform you that there are currently some items that are unavailable or in the process of being obtained or corrected.

[Continue with the rest of the email as previously outlined.]

This approach maintains the respect and professionalism of your communication without needing to specify a name.
Can I put? The Outsourced Doers Talent Acquisition Team

Yes, you can absolutely use "The Outsourced Doers Talent Acquisition Team" as your salutation if your communication is specifically directed to the team handling talent acquisition at Outsourced Doers. This not only personalizes your message to the team but also demonstrates that you are addressing your email to the relevant department within the company. Here is how you could start your email:

Subject: Update on Required Documents Submission

Dear The Outsourced Doers Talent Acquisition Team,

I hope this message finds you well. I am reaching out to provide an update on the status of the required documents for my onboarding process. While I am dedicated to ensuring all necessary paperwork is promptly completed and submitted, I must inform you that there are currently a few documents that are either unavailable or in the process of being obtained or corrected.

[Continue with the rest of the email as previously drafted.]

Using this specific salutation makes your email appear more tailored and shows attention to detail, which is always a positive trait to demonstrate, especially in professional communications.

I am to reply on this

Hi <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>,

The results are in and based on the recruitment process results, you have been selected to be part of a training program provided by Outsourced Doers to potential employees. Well done and Congratulations! We are so excited to offer you an opportunity to train at Outsourced Doers!

Doers Certification: This is a 2-day unpaid qualification where you will be taught some of the necessary skills for the industry. You need to complete this assessment whilst you are completing your requirements. Once you submit the requirements (partial or complete) you will receive another email that has two links (one is a PDF file and the other is a questionnaire) you need to read everything in the PDF file and click the other link to acknowledge the terms and conditions.

Doers Onboarding Training: Our Training Program will be for four (4) weeks with evaluations and examinations each week. Upon passing the Doers Certification, you will move to the 30-day Training Program. This training will provide you with the skills, knowledge, and know-how to be the best possible Digital Marketing Virtual Assistant for your client.

Training Allowance: You shall be provided an allowance with a total of Php 10,000.00 for the 30-day training. After completing the Training program, you may be given an offer to work full-time with Outsourced Doers as a Digital Marketing Virtual Assistant once we find a suitable client for you. You will be sent an employment contract for a potential P 20,000 salary rate with complete statutory benefits (SSS, HDMF, PHIC, 13th-month pay) and several other company-provided benefits such as HMO, Incentives, VL, and SL credits.

Notes:

_Training allowance is not compensation for your training outputs but an allowance you receive during the training period. _
There might be a week or more gap between your training and the matching process.
Basic salary is subject to withholding tax on compensation income. It is the basis of the Philippine Social Security System (“SSS”), Philippine Health Insurance Corporation (“PHIC” or “PhilHealth”), and Home Mutual Development Fund (“HDMF” or “Pag-IBIG”) deductions as required by law.

To proceed with the training, you must submit some documentary requirements through this link: Trainee Information Form

National Bureau of Investigation Clearance (should have at least three months validity before the expiration date. Note: We are not acknowledging the NBI receipt as an official document, however, you may discuss this with the team the expected arrival of the hard copy document)
Certificate of Employment (can be of the following: Signed Resignation Letter with an end date, Exit Clearance Form, or your CoE that should have a start date of employment and an end date. If you are a fresh graduate, with no work experience, kindly indicate on the form "No CoE - Fresh Graduate" or if you are a freelancer, "No CoE - Previous work as a freelancer" on the form.
Birth Certificate (can be any of the following: NSO, PSA, Certificate of Live Birth)
TIN Document (can be any of the following: BIR 1902 form, 1905, 1904, 2305, Verification slip from BIR, Photocopy of TIN ID, ITR-2316 from the previous employer.)
SSS Document (can be any of the following: E1, E4, E6 form, Verification slip from SSS, Member's Statistic Data Form, screenshot from SSS online. (note: we are temporarily not accepting Unified Multi-purpose Id (UMID)).
Pagibig Document (can be any of the following: HDMF or Pag-IBIG ID or Member's Data Form. Your MDF should include your Pag-IBIG Number.)
Philhealth Document (can be any of the following: Philhealth ID or MDR. Your Philhealth ID should have your photo and signature.)
Should you have any questions or concerns, feel free to reach out to <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_EMAIL_ADDRESS>

Congratulations once again and welcome to Outsourced Doers!!

Thank you,

The Outsourced Doers Talent Acquisition Team

Subject: Acceptance and Update on Documentary Requirements for the Training Program

Dear The Outsourced Doers Talent Acquisition Team,

I hope this message finds you well. Firstly, I would like to express my sincere gratitude for being selected to participate in the training program at Outsourced Doers. I am truly excited about the opportunity to develop my skills and contribute to your esteemed company as a Digital Marketing Virtual Assistant.

Regarding the documentary requirements needed for the training, I appreciate the detailed guidance provided. I am currently in the process of gathering the necessary documents and would like to update you on my progress and discuss a few concerns:

1. **Birth Certificate**: I regret to inform you that I currently possess only a photocopy of my birth certificate, as the original has been misplaced. I am in the process of obtaining a new certified copy and will expedite this matter to ensure it is resolved promptly.

2. **SSS Document**: I have secured an SSS document; however, there is a discrepancy that needs to be corrected. I plan to visit the SSS office soon to rectify this issue.

3. **Pag-IBIG Document**: My Pag-IBIG document is currently being processed, and I have a Registration Tracking Number at this stage. I will make the necessary follow-ups to acquire the full Member's Data Form (MDF) as soon as possible.

4. **PhilHealth and NBI Documents**: I intend to process and obtain these documents first thing on Monday, as the offices are not available over the weekend.

5. **TIN Document**: I am pleased to inform you that I have the TIN document ready for submission.

I understand the importance of these documents for starting the training program and assure you of my dedication to completing my requirements in a timely manner. I will submit what I currently have through the provided Trainee Information Form link and update you as I acquire the remaining documents.

Should there be any specific instructions or alternatives regarding my current situation, especially concerning the missing or processing documents, please let me know. I am eager to begin the training and am ready to fulfill any additional requirements or steps necessary.

Thank you once again for this incredible opportunity. I am looking forward to starting my training and embarking on this exciting journey with Outsourced Doers. Please feel free to contact me if further information or discussion is required on my part.

Warm regards,

<PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>
Make it sound as less AI as possible

Subject: Excited to Join the Training Program - A Quick Update on My Documents

Hi there,

First off, wow, thank you so much for this opportunity! I'm beyond excited to dive into the training program at Outsourced Doers and to ultimately become part of the team as a Digital Marketing Virtual Assistant. It's exactly the kind of challenge I've been looking for, and I'm eager to get started.

I've gone through the list of documents you need from me, and I'm on it. Just wanted to keep you in the loop about where things stand:

- **Birth Certificate**: Running into a tiny hiccup here, as I only have a photocopy. The original got lost in the shuffle a while back. I'm on a mission to get a new official copy and will handle this as quickly as possible.

- **SSS Document**: Got one, but there's a small mistake that needs fixing. I’ll head to the SSS office to get this sorted out.

- **Pag-IBIG Document**: This one's in process. Right now, I've got the Registration Tracking Number and am following up to get the full MDF.

- **PhilHealth and NBI Documents**: Planning to sort these out first thing on Monday. It's on my calendar.

- **TIN Document**: This one's good to go! Ready to be submitted whenever you are.

I totally get how crucial these documents are and I want to assure you that I'm doing everything I can to get this squared away fast. I'll submit what I've got now through the Trainee Information Form link and will keep you updated as I tick each of these off the list.

If there's a workaround or any additional steps I need to take given my current document situation, just let me know. I'm all about finding solutions and making sure we're good to go.

Again, thanks a ton for this opportunity. Can't wait to get started and see where this journey takes us. If you need anything more from my side, just shout. I'll be here.

Best,

<PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>

2. SSS Document: I have secured an SSS document; however, there is a discrepancy that needs to be corrected. I plan to visit the SSS office soon to rectify this issue.

Can you change this into saying that there is an error in my name and am planning to rectify it as soon as possible
- **SSS Document**: There's a little snag with my SSS document - an error in my name, of all things. But no worries, I'm planning to swing by the SSS office ASAP to get this all straightened out.
Keep the previous version

Dear The Outsourced Doers Talent Acquisition Team,

First off, thank you so much for this opportunity! I’m beyond excited to dive into the training program at Outsourced Doers and to ultimately become part of the team as a Digital Marketing Virtual Assistant. It’s exactly the kind of challenge I’ve been looking for, and I’m eager to get started.

Regarding the documentary requirements needed for the training, I appreciate the detailed guidance provided. I am currently in the process of gathering the necessary documents and would like to update you on my progress and discuss a few concerns:

1. Birth Certificate: I regret to inform you that I currently possess only a photocopy of my birth certificate, as the original has been misplaced. I am in the process of obtaining a new certified copy and will expedite this matter to ensure it is resolved promptly.

2. SSS Document: I have secured an SSS document; however, there is a discrepancy that needs to be corrected. I plan to visit the SSS office soon to rectify this issue.

3. Pag-IBIG Document: My Pag-IBIG document is currently being processed, and I have a Registration Tracking Number at this stage. I will make the necessary follow-ups to acquire the full Member’s Data Form (MDF) as soon as possible.

4. PhilHealth and NBI Documents: I intend to process and obtain these documents first thing on Monday, as the offices are not available over the weekend.

5. TIN Document: I am pleased to inform you that I have the TIN document ready for submission.

I understand the importance of these documents for starting the training program and assure you of my dedication to completing my requirements in a timely manner. I will submit what I currently have through the provided Trainee Information Form link and update you as I acquire the remaining documents.

Should there be any specific instructions or alternatives regarding my current situation, especially concerning the missing or processing documents, please let me know. I am eager to begin the training and am ready to fulfill any additional requirements or steps necessary.

Thank you once again for this incredible opportunity. I am looking forward to starting my training and embarking on this exciting journey with Outsourced Doers. Please feel free to contact me if further information or discussion is required on my part.

Warm regards,

<PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>
